heading: Early identification of needed improvements in financial management
label: 1831m Early identification of needed improvements in financial management

children.heading: Annual report on financial condition and management
children.label: a Annual report on financial condition and management

children.heading: Report required
children.label: 1 Report required
children.text: Each insured depository institution shall submit an annual report to the Corporation, the appropriate Federal banking agency, and any appropriate State bank supervisor (including any State bank supervisor of a host State).

children.label: i
children.text: the institution’s management under subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: an independent public accountant under subsections (c) and (d); and

children.label: B
children.text: such other information as the Corporation and the appropriate Federal banking agency may determine to be necessary to assess the financial condition and management of the institution.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 3 Public availability
children.text: Any annual report required under paragraph (1) shall be available for public inspection. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, the Corporation and the appropriate Federal banking agencies may designate certain information as privileged and confidential and not available to the public.

children.heading: Management responsibility for financial statements and internal controls
children.label: b Management responsibility for financial statements and internal controls

children.text: annual financial statements in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and such other disclosure requirements as the Corporation and the appropriate Federal banking agency may prescribe; and

children.label: i
children.text: preparing financial statements;

children.label: ii
children.text: establishing and maintaining an adequate internal control structure and procedures for financial reporting; and

children.label: iii
children.text: complying with the laws and regulations relating to safety and soundness which are designated by the Corporation and the appropriate Federal banking agency; and

children.label: i
children.text: the effectiveness of such internal control structure and procedures; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the institution’s compliance with the laws and regulations relating to safety and soundness which are designated by the Corporation and the appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.heading: Internal control evaluation and reporting requirements for independent public accountants
children.label: c Internal control evaluation and reporting requirements for independent public accountants

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to any internal control report required by subsection (b)(2) of any institution, the institution’s independent public accountant shall attest to, and report separately on, the assertions of the institution’s management contained in such report.

children.heading: Attestation requirements
children.label: 2 Attestation requirements
children.text: Any attestation pursuant to paragraph (1) shall be made in accordance with generally accepted standards for attestation engagements.

children.heading: Annual independent audits of financial statements
children.label: d Annual independent audits of financial statements

children.heading: Audits required
children.label: 1 Audits required
children.text: The Corporation, in consultation with the appropriate Federal banking agencies, shall prescribe regulations requiring that each insured depository institution shall have an annual independent audit made of the institution’s financial statements by an independent public accountant in accordance with generally accepted auditing standards and .section 1831n of this title

children.label: A
children.text: are presented fairly in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles; and

children.label: B
children.text: comply with such other disclosure requirements as the Corporation and the appropriate Federal banking agency may prescribe.

children.heading: Requirements for insured subsidiaries of holding companies
children.label: 3 Requirements for insured subsidiaries of holding companies
children.text: The requirements for an independent audit under this subsection may be satisfied for insured depository institutions that are subsidiaries of a holding company by an independent audit of the holding company.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 104–208, div. A, title II, § 2301(a)110 Stat. 3009–419Sept. 30, 1996
children.label: e Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 104–208, div. A, title II, § 2301(a)110 Stat. 3009–419Sept. 30, 1996

children.heading: Form and content of reports and auditing standards
children.label: f Form and content of reports and auditing standards

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The scope of each report by an independent public accountant pursuant to this section, and the procedures followed in preparing such report, shall meet or exceed the scope and procedures required by generally accepted auditing standards and other applicable standards recognized by the Corporation.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: The Corporation shall consult with the other appropriate Federal banking agencies in implementing this subsection.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: Each insured depository institution (to which this section applies) shall have an independent audit committee entirely made up of outside directors who are independent of management of the institution, except as provided in subparagraph (D), and who satisfy any specific requirements the Corporation may establish.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: B Duties
children.text: An independent audit committee’s duties shall include reviewing with management and the independent public accountant the basis for the reports issued under subsections (b)(2), (c), and (d).

children.heading: Criteria applicable to committees of large insured depository institutions
children.label: C Criteria applicable to committees of large insured depository institutions
i
children.text: include members with banking or related financial management expertise;

children.label: ii
children.text: have access to the committee’s own outside counsel; and

children.label: iii
children.text: not include any large customers of the institution.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An appropriate Federal banking agency may, by order or regulation, permit the independent audit committee of an insured depository institution to be made up of less than all, but no fewer than a majority of, outside directors, if the agency determines that the institution has encountered hardships in retaining and recruiting a sufficient number of competent outside directors to serve on the internal audit committee of the institution.

children.heading: Factors to be considered
children.label: ii Factors to be considered
children.text: In determining whether an insured depository institution has encountered hardships referred to in clause (i), the appropriate Federal banking agency shall consider factors such as the size of the institution, and whether the institution has made a good faith effort to elect or name additional competent outside directors to the board of directors of the institution who may serve on the internal audit committee.

children.heading: Review of quarterly reports of large insured depository institutions
children.label: 2 Review of quarterly reports of large insured depository institutions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any insured depository institution which the Corporation has determined to be a large institution, the Corporation may require the independent public accountant retained by such institution to perform reviews of the institution’s quarterly financial reports in accordance with procedures agreed upon by the Corporation.

children.heading: Report to audit committee
children.label: B Report to audit committee
children.text: The independent public accountant referred to in subparagraph (A) shall provide the audit committee of the insured depository institution with reports on the reviews under such subparagraph and the audit committee shall provide such reports to the Corporation, any appropriate Federal banking agency, and any appropriate State bank supervisor.

children.heading: Limitation on notice
children.label: C Limitation on notice
children.text: Reports provided under subparagraph (B) shall be only for the information and use of the insured depository institution, the Corporation, any appropriate Federal banking agency, and any State bank supervisor that received the report.

children.heading: Notice to institution
children.label: D Notice to institution
children.text: The Corporation shall promptly notify an insured depository institution, in writing, of a determination pursuant to subparagraph (A) to require a review of such institution’s quarterly financial reports.

children.label: i
children.text: has agreed to provide related working papers, policies, and procedures to the Corporation, any appropriate Federal banking agency, and any State bank supervisor, if requested; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has received a peer review that meets guidelines acceptable to the Corporation.

children.heading: Reports on peer reviews
children.label: B Reports on peer reviews
children.text: Reports on peer reviews shall be filed with the Corporation and made available for public inspection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In addition to any authority contained in , the Corporation or an appropriate Federal banking agency may remove, suspend, or bar an independent public accountant, upon a showing of good cause, from performing audit services required by this section.section 1818 of this title

children.heading: Joint rulemaking
children.label: B Joint rulemaking
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agencies shall jointly issue rules of practice to implement this paragraph.

children.heading: Notice by accountant of termination of services
children.label: 5 Notice by accountant of termination of services
children.text: Any independent public accountant performing an audit under this section who subsequently ceases to be the accountant for the institution shall promptly notify the Corporation and each appropriate Federal banking agency pursuant to such rules as the Corporation and each appropriate Federal banking agency shall prescribe.

children.heading: Exchange of reports and information
children.label: h Exchange of reports and information

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each insured depository institution which has engaged the services of an independent auditor to audit such institution shall transmit to the auditor a copy of the most recent report of condition made by the institution (pursuant to this chapter or any other provision of law) and a copy of the most recent report of examination received by the institution.

children.label: i
children.text: a copy of any supervisory memorandum of understanding with such institution and any written agreement between such institution and any appropriate Federal banking agency or any appropriate State bank supervisor which is in effect during the period covered by the audit; and

children.label: I
children.text: any action initiated or taken by the appropriate Federal banking agency or the Corporation during such period under subsection (a), (b), (c), (e), (g), (i), (s), or (t) of ;section 1818 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: any action taken by any appropriate State bank supervisor under State law which is similar to any action referred to in subclause (I); or

children.label: III
children.text: any assessment of any civil money penalty under any other provision of law with respect to the institution or any institution-affiliated party.

children.heading: Independent auditor reports
children.label: A Independent auditor reports
children.text: Each insured depository institution shall provide to the Corporation, any appropriate Federal banking agency, and any appropriate State bank supervisor, a copy of each audit report and any qualification to such report, any management letter, and any other report within 15 days of receipt of any such report, qualification, or letter from the institution’s independent auditors.

children.heading: Notice of change of auditor
children.label: B Notice of change of auditor
children.text: Each insured depository institution shall provide written notification to the Corporation, the appropriate Federal banking agency, and any appropriate State bank supervisor of the resignation or dismissal of the institution’s independent auditor or the engagement of a new independent auditor by the institution, including a statement of the reasons for such change within 15 calendar days of the occurrence of the event.

children.heading: Requirements for insured subsidiaries of holding companies
children.label: i Requirements for insured subsidiaries of holding companies
A
children.text: services and functions comparable to those required under this section are provided at the holding company level; and

children.label: i
children.text: has total assets, as of the beginning of such fiscal year, of less than $5,000,000,000; or

children.label: I
children.text: total assets, as of the beginning of such fiscal year, of $5,000,000,000, or more; and

children.label: II
children.text: a CAMEL composite rating of 1 or 2 under the Uniform Financial Institutions Rating System (or an equivalent rating by any such agency under a comparable rating system) as of the most recent examination of such institution by the Corporation or the appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.heading: Large institutions
children.label: 2 Large institutions
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, in the case of an insured depository institution described in paragraph (1)(B)(ii) that the Corporation determines to be a large institution, the audit committee of the holding company of such an institution shall not include any large customers of the institution.

children.heading: Applicability based on risk to fund
children.label: 3 Applicability based on risk to fund
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agency may require an institution with total assets in excess of $9,000,000,000 to comply with this section, notwithstanding the exemption provided by this subsection, if it determines that such exemption would create a significant risk to the Deposit Insurance Fund if applied to that institution.

children.heading: Exemption for small depository institutions
children.label: j Exemption for small depository institutions

children.text: such amount (in excess of $150,000,000) as the Corporation may prescribe by regulation.
